finding.summary: True peak measures -0.1 dBFS, leaving almost no headroom for inter-sample peaks; on a parallel drum bus that will typically be summed with the dry drum signal downstream, this is a real clipping risk. Sample peak is similarly hot at -0.3 dBFS. The track has no FX chain (fx_chain is empty) and no clip protection/limiter in place, and track volume is unity (0.0 dB).
finding.probable_cause: Track volume at 0.0 dB combined with hot source material and no limiting or safety processing in the FX chain (fx_chain is empty). For a parallel drum bus intended to be summed with a dry signal, this leaves insufficient headroom for the sum and risks inter-sample clipping downstream.
finding.confidence_reason: true_peak_db is a measured, non-null value directly indicating the issue. Downstream summing topology (master bus limiter, parallel summing gain) is not visible from this single-track payload, so the practical impact depends on context not shown here.

evidence_refs.description: Measured true peak of -0.1 dBFS, within 0.1 dB of digital full scale
Sample peak of -0.3 dBFS, consistent with a hot signal
Track fader at 0.0 dB, contributing to the hot output level
Empty FX chain means no limiter/clip protection is inserted

proposal.reason: Preview changing track volume from 0.000 dB to -1.000 dB to test whether true_peak_db decreases.
